phase1.explanation:
🎉 Congratulations! You've completed Unit 2 — OOP Deep Dive! Let's review what you've learned and see where we're going next.

--- Unit 2 Recap ---

1. Inheritance — reuse code with extends
   • IS-A relationship
   • super keyword for parent access

2. Method Overriding — provide specific implementations
   • @Override annotation
   • Runtime polymorphism

3. Polymorphism — many forms
   • Compile-time (overloading)
   • Runtime (overriding)
   • Upcasting and downcasting

4. Abstract Classes — partial blueprints
   • abstract methods without body
   • Can have concrete methods and fields

5. Interfaces — full contracts
   • implements multiple
   • Default and static methods (Java 8+)

6. Packages — organize code
   • import statements
   • Reverse domain naming

7. Static — class-level members
   • Shared across all instances
   • Static blocks and methods

8. Final — no changes allowed
   • Final variables, methods, classes

9. Inner Classes — classes inside classes
   • Non-static, static, local, anonymous

10. Enums — fixed set of constants
    • Can have fields and methods
    • Type-safe

11. Object Class — root of all classes
    • toString(), equals(), hashCode()

12. Wrappers — primitives as objects
    • Autoboxing and unboxing

--- What's Next? Design Patterns! ---

You've learned the tools of OOP. Now learn how to use them effectively!

Design Patterns are reusable solutions to common problems:

Strategy Pattern — enables swapping algorithms at runtime
• Instead of hardcoding behavior, use interfaces
• Example: Payment methods (CreditCard, PayPal, Crypto)

Composition over Inheritance:
• Favor has-a over is-a
• More flexible, easier to test

You'll see these patterns in action in future projects!

Java Ecosystem Map:
• Core Java (you're here!)
• Java EE / Jakarta (enterprise)
• Spring Framework (most popular)
• Android (mobile)
• Desktop (JavaFX, Swing)

Keep going — you're building a solid foundation! 💪
phase1.openingDialogue: Unit 2 complete! Let's review everything and see what's next~ 🎉

phase2.explanation:
Conceptual review — no broken code here!

Common misconception: "Inheritance is always the best choice for code reuse."

Actually, composition is often better:
• More flexible
• Avoids tight coupling
• Easier to test

Example of composition over inheritance:
Instead of: class Car extends Engine
Use: class Car { Engine engine; }

Design patterns help you make the right choice!

phase3.prompt:
Which statement about OOP concepts is TRUE?

A) A class can extend multiple classes (multiple inheritance)
B) Interfaces can have concrete methods using default keyword (Java 8+)
C) Abstract classes can't have constructors
D) The equals() method compares object references by default
E) Static methods can access instance variables
F) final classes can be extended

Select the CORRECT statement.
dialogueHints: Check each option carefully — only one is correct.
phase3.openingDialogue: Final check! Which of these do you know? 🤔
